definitions: code that doesn't wait, runs in background
client is the device/program that makes requests to a server, basically, the 'thing' that's asking for services
frontend is the code/interface that runs on the client that the user sees and interacts with

types.name: examples of clients
types.description: your browser (chrome, firefox), your extension (the program running in browser), a mobile app, even another server can be a client

types.name: examples of frontend
types.description: html/css/js files, ui/design, buttons, forms, visuals

note: client = hardware/program (the thing making requests). frontend = the code running on it (what the user sees). server (computer/program) → backend (code on server). client (browser/device) → frontend (code in browser)
